findings.title: README omits all mention of the unit test suite after e2e removal
findings.detail: The PR removes all e2e infrastructure and the README's Scripts table is updated accordingly — but it never mentions `npm run test:unit`, the 19 unit test files that remain in src/, or the testing posture a forker should adopt. A forker reading only the README has no signal that unit tests exist, how to run them, or what they cover. This is a documentation gap that undermines the stated goal of making the template coherent for forkers.
findings.recommendation: Add `test:unit` to the Scripts table in README.md with a one-line description. A short note like 'Ships with 19 unit tests for core utilities and hooks; forkers are expected to add their own integration/e2e tests against their own backend' makes the posture explicit.

findings.title: Unchecked test plan item: fresh-clone dev boot
findings.detail: The PR body lists 'Verify npm run dev boots cleanly on a fresh clone' as an unchecked checkbox. The PR also removes `.githooks/pre-commit` and `scripts/stamp-version.sh`. If the pre-commit hook was doing any build-time validation that surfaces on `npm run dev` (e.g., it ran `generate:types`), removing it without verifying the fresh-clone path could leave a broken first-run experience. This is the most plausible real risk from the unchecked item.
findings.recommendation: Run `npm run dev` after a fresh `git clone + npm install` (or confirm in a CI matrix job) before merging. The risk is low given the hook was version-stamping only, but the checkbox exists for a reason.
